query: recent studies on erythropoietin neuroprotection in preterm infants

retrieved_docs.title: Epoetin Alfa -- Indications -- Experimental Uses
retrieved_docs.content: Neuroprotection in preterm infants: Erythropoietin is hypothesized to have neuroprotective effects in preterm infants. A 2020 randomized controlled trial found that epoetin alfa was not associated with improved mortality or reduced severe neurodevelopmental impairment by the age of 2 years. [19]

retrieved_docs.title: Erythropoietin Stimulating Agents -- Administration -- Pediatric patients:
retrieved_docs.content: In a randomized multicenter study, preterm infants receiving darbepoetin or EPO showed improved cognitive outcomes at 18 to 22 months compared to placebo. These ESAs were associated with higher cognitive scores and better cognitive permanence. [19] Epoetin alfa is recommended for patients 1 month and older with CKD-associated anemia. However, its safety and effectiveness have not been established for patients younger than 1 month. Epoetin alfa is recommended for patients 5 and older with chemotherapy-related anemia, but its safety and efficacy have not been established for patients younger than 5. Darbepoetin alfa is indicated for patients with CKD who are 1 month or older. However, its safety and effectiveness have not been established for patients younger than 1 month, and its use in pediatric cancer has not been established.

retrieved_docs.title: Epoetin Alfa -- Indications -- Experimental Uses
retrieved_docs.content: Anemia of prematurity: Blood transfusions commonly treat anemia in preterm infants. However, a 2019 Cochrane meta-analysis found that epoetin alfa had a minimal effect on reducing the number of blood transfusions and did not decrease mortality in preterm infants. [18]

retrieved_docs.title: Erythropoietin Stimulating Agents -- Administration -- Breastfeeding considerations:
retrieved_docs.content: The excretion of darbepoetin alfa into breast milk and its effects on breastfed infants have not been specifically studied. Darbepoetin alfa is immunologically and biologically similar to native EPO. Intravenous administration of darbepoetin alfa to newborn infants has been safely conducted using doses exceeding those anticipated to be excreted into breast milk. Therefore, no special precautions are necessary while breastfeeding. [17] The excretion of methoxy polyethylene glycol-epoetin β into breast milk has not been studied. Due to the lack of specific data on methoxy polyethylene glycol-epoetin β in breastfeeding, an alternative medication may be preferable, particularly when nursing a newborn or preterm infant. [18]

retrieved_docs.title: Neonatal Therapeutic Hypothermia -- Continuing Education Activity
retrieved_docs.content: Therapeutic hypothermia has emerged as a standard intervention for neonates with moderate-to-severe hypoxic-ischemic encephalopathy, as extensive research demonstrates its neuroprotective benefits in reducing brain injury and improving survival with fewer neurological complications. However, critical questions remain regarding optimal patient selection, timing, and efficacy in specific populations, such as preterm infants and neonates in resource-limited settings.

retrieved_docs.title: Retinopathy of Prematurity -- Pearls and Other Issues -- Pearls and Other Issues
retrieved_docs.content: Anti-VEGF therapy: Pros and cons

retrieved_docs.title: Retinopathy of Prematurity -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Multiple risk factors contribute to the development of ROP. The most significant include low gestational age, low birth weight, and the need for supplemental oxygen. Additional contributors include prolonged mechanical ventilation, sepsis, intraventricular hemorrhage, blood transfusions, and poor postnatal weight gain. The advent of the STOP-ROP (Supplemental Therapeutic Oxygen for Prethreshold–Retinopathy of Prematurity), WINROP (Weight, Insulin-like Growth Factor, Neonatal Retinopathy of Prematurity), and e-ROP (Telemedicine Approaches to Evaluating Acute-Phase Retinopathy of Prematurity) models has clarified how systemic risk factors and early biomarkers can predict disease progression, aiding in individualized screening strategies. [8]

retrieved_docs.title: Erythropoietin Stimulating Agents -- Indications
retrieved_docs.content: ESAs are generally indicated for patients with impaired red blood cell production conditions. The 2 primary FDA-approved indications for ESA administration are 1) anemia secondary to chronic kidney disease (CKD) and 2) chemotherapy-induced anemia in patients with cancer. [2] The FDA approved using epoetin (1993) and darbepoetin (2002) for patients with chemotherapy-induced anemia. The FDA has also approved ESAs for the treatment of anemia secondary to zidovudine therapy for HIV infection, patients receiving autologous blood transfusions, patients with anemia undergoing elective surgery (pre-op and post-op), and anemia in preterm infants. ESAs have also demonstrated therapeutic benefits for patients with CKD, characterized by renal damage, and reduced EPO production by peritubular cells. [3] ESAs are beneficial for patients receiving dialysis or who need dialysis. In patients with CKD and chemotherapy-induced anemia, ESAs are generally reserved for patients with a hemoglobin <10 g/dL due to the risk of adverse effects. [2]

retrieved_docs.title: Retinopathy of Prematurity -- Pertinent Studies and Ongoing Trials -- Highlights from Recent Literature
retrieved_docs.content: AI and telemedicine : Rapid developments in AI models for ROP screening (eg, i-ROP deep learning) are enhancing detection in low-resource settings. [107] VEGF suppression and systemic safety : Studies have highlighted systemic VEGF suppression after anti-VEGF therapy in neonates, prompting the need for careful monitoring. [108] Long-term follow-up : Recent research emphasizes the importance of a 3- to 5-year follow-up after anti-VEGF injections, considering the risks of late recurrence. [109]

retrieved_docs.title: Erythropoietin Stimulating Agents -- Contraindications
retrieved_docs.content: Epoetin alfa, darbepoetin, and methoxy polyethylene glycol-epoetin β are contraindicated for patients with uncontrolled hypertension, pure red cell aplasia (PRCA), and severe allergic reactions. EPO-stimulating agents are contraindicated for patients with hypersensitivity to non-human mammal-derived products because of ESA production methods. [3] ESAs containing benzyl alcohol are contraindicated for neonates, peripartum mothers, and breastfeeding mothers due to the risk of gasping syndrome. This syndrome causes gasping respiration, renal failure, and neurological deterioration in neonates, resulting from severe metabolic acidosis. [26]

retrieved_docs.title: Epoetin Alfa -- Adverse Effects
retrieved_docs.content: Benzyl alcohol, used as a preservative in multiple-dose vials of epoetin alfa, is toxic to neonates, potentially causing a fatal "gasping syndrome" characterized by neurological and metabolic disturbances and gasping respirations. [38] Epoetin alfa formulations often contain human albumin, posing a theoretical risk of transmitting infectious diseases.

retrieved_docs.title: Retinopathy of Prematurity -- History and Physical -- Neonatal Intensive Care Unit Course
retrieved_docs.content: Multiple transfusions introduce adult hemoglobin, which reduces oxygen affinity and impairs tissue oxygenation. Iron overload from transfusions can increase oxidative stress.

retrieved_docs.title: Carboxyhemoglobin Toxicity -- Treatment / Management
retrieved_docs.content: Erythropoietin (EPO) is an emerging novel therapy for the treatment of carbon monoxide toxicity. Erythropoietin is of interest since it has been shown in animal models to have neuronal protective effects in global and local ischemia. One study on CO toxicity showed decreased delayed neurologic sequelae versus placebo; however, studies regarding EPO use in ischemic strokes have shown higher death rates, and additional research is necessary before this can be considered.

retrieved_docs.title: Retinopathy of Prematurity -- Treatment / Management
retrieved_docs.content: The beneficial effects of VEGF, a hormone that is both neurotrophic and neuroprotective, should not be overlooked, as it helps maintain the blood-brain barrier, generates surfactant in the lungs, facilitates the formation of glomeruli in the kidneys, and stimulates skeletal growth. [17] Results from a 2019 study demonstrated higher mortality and poor cognitive outcomes in infants treated with bevacizumab than with other treatment modalities. [70] Retinal detachment carries a significantly higher risk of a poor visual outcome. [71] [72] [73] [74] [75] For this reason, in advanced ROP, surgery is indicated for stages 4A, 4B, and 5. Modalities for surgical intervention in stage 4 ROP include scleral buckling or lens-sparing vitrectomy.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 14 -- Level 3 -- Community 1707
retrieved_docs.content: Neonatal disorder (اضطراب لدى حديث الولادة) is a broad category of conditions affecting newborns. Respiratory distress syndrome is identified as a specific type of neonatal disorder, and the category also includes the child conditions fetal erythroblastosis (كثرة الأرومات الحمر الجنينية) and premature rupture of membranes (رضح الولادة). Prevention and control measures are applicable to the overall neonatal disorder as well as to each of its constituent conditions—respiratory distress syndrome, fetal erythroblastosis, and premature rupture of membranes—underscoring the importance of proactive management in newborn care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 12 -- Level 0 -- Community 12
retrieved_docs.content: Query “disorders of the newborn” (SMQ) includes extreme prematurity (“birth at the highest degree of prematurity, unspecified weight”), intracranial hemorrhage, and unspecified perinatal infection, reinforcing the interconnectedness of extreme prematurity, fetal growth restriction, perinatal infection, hemorrhagic and hemolytic disorders, and birth‑related trauma. These hierarchical classifications support diagnosis, risk stratification, and targeted therapeutic interventions throughout the perinatal and early infant periods.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 4 -- Community 2825
retrieved_docs.content: INFANT PRETERM LOW BIRTH WEIGHT is clinically associated with قصر القامة (short stature), indicating that infants born preterm with low birth weight often experience growth restriction that results in reduced adult height. In turn, قصر القامة is clinically associated with وفاة حديث الولادة (neonatal death), suggesting that short stature in this context reflects severe underlying health challenges that increase the risk of death shortly after birth. Recognizing the link between low‑birth‑weight preterm infants, subsequent short stature, and heightened neonatal mortality can guide clinicians in early monitoring and intervention strategies to improve outcomes for this vulnerable population.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 29 -- Level 3 -- Community 1830
retrieved_docs.content: اضطراب لدى حديث الولادة (neonatal disorder) is a broad clinical category that includes specific conditions such as respiratory distress syndrome and كثرة الأرومات الحمر الجنينية (excess fetal red erythroblasts). The inverse_isa relationship indicates that respiratory distress syndrome is a subtype of اضطراب لدى حديث الولادة, while the has child relationship shows that كثرة الأرومات الحمر الجنينية is a manifestation or sub‑condition within the same neonatal disorder. For each of these entities—اضطراب لدى حديث الولادة, respiratory distress syndrome, and كثرة الأرومات الحمر الجنينية—the qualifier “etiologie” is allowed, meaning that the cause or etiology can be specified for each condition. Recognizing and describing the etiological factors of the neonatal disorder and its constituent conditions is essential for accurate diagnosis and targeted management in newborn care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 22 -- Level 4 -- Community 2763
retrieved_docs.content: Short gestation, low birth weight, and fetal growth retardation constitute a severe perinatal condition that is classified as an extremely premature birth of the highest degree with unspecified weight (ولادة بأقصى درجات الابتسار، غير محددة {الوزن}). This classification highlights that the infant is born far before term, exhibits markedly reduced body weight, and shows evidence of impaired intra‑uterine growth, all of which place the newborn at high risk for morbidity and mortality and necessitate intensive neonatal monitoring and intervention.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 12 -- Level 0 -- Community 12
retrieved_docs.content: entities. Hemolytic disease of the newborn and neonatal hemorrhage are subcategories of both hemolytic jaundice and perinatal jaundice; laboratory findings of low total hemoglobin aid assessment of hemolysis severity. Cerebral injury due to birth trauma is an inverse‑ISA manifestation of birth trauma, illustrating a direct link between traumatic delivery and neonatal intracerebral hemorrhage. Within ICD‑10, “Certain conditions originating in the perinatal period” (P00‑P96) encompasses “Respiratory and cardiovascular disorders specific to the perinatal period” (P19‑P29) and shares the parent “short gestation; low birth weight; fetal growth retardation,” highlighting overlap between respiratory/cardiovascular complications and growth‑related perinatal disorders. The Standardized MedDRA Query “disorders of the newborn” (SMQ) includes extreme prematurity (“birth at the highest degree of prematurity, unspecified weight”), intracranial hemorrhage, and unspecified perinatal infection,

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 8 -- Level 3 -- Community 2605
retrieved_docs.content: INFANT PRETERM LOW BIRTH WEIGHT is clinically associated with قصر القامة (short stature), indicating that infants born preterm with low birth weight often develop reduced linear growth. In turn, قصر القامة is clinically associated with وفاة حديث الولادة (neonatal death), suggesting that short stature in this vulnerable population may be linked to an increased risk of death shortly after birth. The Pediatric Adverse Events Terminology encompasses adverse pediatric outcomes, and its subset includes the concepts تدلّي الحبل السري (umbilical cord prolapse) and وفاة حديث الولادة (neonatal death). Together, these relationships highlight a network of interrelated risk factors and adverse events: preterm low birth weight, short stature, and neonatal death are closely connected, while umbilical cord prolapse is also recognized within the same adverse‑event framework, underscoring the importance of comprehensive monitoring and intervention in high‑risk newborns.

Consequently, extreme prematurity, birth‑related trauma leading to intracranial hemorrhage, and perinatal infections constitute interrelated components of neonatal morbidity, informing clinicians about the spectrum of conditions to consider when evaluating and managing newborn patients.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 2 -- Community 943
retrieved_docs.content: Certain conditions originating in the perinatal period (P00‑P96) are situated within the broader category of Short gestation; low birth weight; and fetal growth retardation, which is classified as ولادة بأقصى درجات الابتسار، غير محددة {الوزن}. Within the perinatal‑originating conditions, there is a more specific subgroup of Respiratory and cardiovascular disorders specific to the perinatal period (P19‑P29). This hierarchical organization indicates that infants born with extreme prematurity and unspecified low birth weight are at risk for a range of perinatal conditions, including distinct respiratory and cardiovascular disorders, highlighting the importance of recognizing the underlying gestational and growth‑related factors when assessing and managing newborn health.

The disorder “Disorder relating to short gestation AND/OR low birthweight” is both a child of liveborn and an occurrence within the Neonatal qualifier, emphasizing the heightened risk of neonatal‑specific complications in preterm or low‑birth‑weight infants. In the SMQ “اضطرابات لدى حديث الولادة”, three entities are explicitly enumerated: “ولادة بأقصى درجات الابتسار، غير محددة {الوزن}” (birth at the highest gestational age with unspecified weight), “نزيف مخي وليدي” (intracranial and possibly spinal hemorrhage in the newborn), and “عدوى في الفترة المحيطة بالولادة، غير محدد بخلاف ذلك” (unspecified perinatal infection). Recognizing these entities as members of the SMQ enables systematic identification, coding, and reporting of important newborn disorders.
